Patch Set 11: Code-Review+1

Now that local and peer nis can each have a different timeout, it might be nice if lnet_recover_*_nis() returned the time that the next recovery attempt was needed, and then lnet_monitor_thread() could set recovery_timeout more precisely.

But I doubt it really matters, and maybe there is reason to believe it wouldn't even be correct....

commit.subject: LU-13569 lnet: Recover local NI w/exponential backoff interval
commit.message:
LU-13569 lnet: Recover local NI w/exponential backoff interval

Use an exponential backoff algorithm to determine the interval at
which unhealthy local NIs are ping'd

Introduce lnet_ni_add_to_recoveryq_locked() which handles checking
pre-conditions for whether the NI should be added to the recovery
queue, and takes a ref on the NI as appropriate.

Test-Parameters: trivial
HPE-bug-id: LUS-9109
Signed-off-by: Chris Horn <chris.horn@hpe.com>
Change-Id: Idb3789366b2e450837989f9a12eb2d598f80081c
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/39721
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Alexander Boyko <alexander.boyko@hpe.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
